content:
Hi Tombarkas

What do you mean "It makes no difference". Could you detail what your exceptions are versus what your observing.

Unified inbox folder

The only area where the action is limited is within the unified inbox folder. There you must open the email first before moving it. Moving from the message list is not available.

  1. Open email
  2. Press 3 dot menu
  3. Select Move, or refile > Move

For other accounts & folders

From the message list view

  1. Select message(s)
  2. Press 3 dot menu
  3. Select move.

From an open email.

  1. Press 3 dot menu
  2. Select Move, or refile > Move

Swipe

The swipe operation also appears to work for the move operation. Swiping displays folder list allowing you to choose where to move the email to.

content:
Hi relativevalue1

To avoid accidental deletions, the app can be setup to prompt for confirmation before any deletion. To enable this, navigate to settings > general settings: Confirm Actions. Select delete items.

Can you check you have a trash/deleted folder assigned under settings > account > folders. Emails will normally be moved into the trash folder as long the folder is assigned.

Under settings > account > fetching mail, there's a number of settings allowing you to set how deletions should be handled on the server. You can review these and change to fit with your usage.

Q) Are you using an Imap or pop3 connection to the server.

content:
The Thunderbird app doesn't store emails and data on their own server/cloud . It connects to your email provider's server/cloud. For example Gmail, Microsoft, Yahoo etc.

Who is your email provider?

If you used Thunderbird Mobile on your old phone, and connected using the IMAP Protocol your emails will still be on your email provider's servers. Once you add your account to the app, your emails will download.

To add an account

  1. Open the mobile app.
  2. Click start.
  3. Click add an account now.
  4. Enter your email address.
  5. Click next.

Thunderbird will try and automatically detect the connection settings. If successful ...

  1. Enter your password, or if using oAuth authentication, click Sign in with [provider] button.
  2. Complete the login steps
  3. Upon successful authentication, your emails will download.

If you used the Pop3 protocol on your old phone, your emails might be lost unless you configured your email provider settings to keep a copy on the server. Pop3 stores emails on the local phone/PC, not the server by default.

content:
You don't say if your email client is Thunderbird mobile or desktop!

If you use Thunderbird's desktop client, it features built in spam filtering capability. You can find a guide here.

Most email providers also support server side filtering, typically configured on their webmail settings page. You can add a rule to automatically delete or move emails into the spam folder, based on criteria such as keyword or email address. Rules applied server side are reflected on all clients including webmail, desktop and mobile.

Thunderbird for Android doesn't provide built-in email filtering, so server side spam rules are better suited when mobile is used.

content:
If your question relates to Thunderbird Android,

  1. Open the settings page.
  2. Select your gmail account.
  3. Press the 3 dot menu, top right on the page.
  4. Press remove account.

You'll receive desktop specific support on their forum linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/thunderbird

I'm not familiar with the desktop version, AI answer's as follows.

Steps to remove an account Open Thunderbird and go to Account Settings:

  1. Use the menu (≡) > Settings > Account Settings, or look under Tools/Edit > Account Settings depending on your version.
  2. ​In the left pane, click the email account you want to remove (click the top line with the email address, not just its subfolders).
  3. ​At the bottom of the Account Settings window, click Account Actions and choose Remove Account (or Delete in some versions).
  4. ​In the confirmation dialog, choose whether to remove only the account settings or also delete message data stored on your computer, then click Remove/OK to confirm.
  5. ​Close the dialog and restart Thunderbird if prompted or if the account list does not refresh immediately.

content:
You can change this behaviour in Thunderbird Android.

Navigate to settings > account > reading mail: always show images The setting has 3 options, No (default), From contacts, From anyone.

Set the option "From contacts" and add the individual's email into your Android contacts list. Images on their emails should then automatically download.

The default value is unlikely to change, being the more secure privacy focused option.

To enable the contacts feature 1. Check you've granted the app permission to read your contacts. Long press the app icon > app info > permissions. 2. Open the app settings > general settings > display. Enable "Show correspondent names" and "Show contact names"

content:
As it stands, some features you may require are unavailable in the current Thunderbird for Android version (v15).

The teams are currently redesigning the user interface and have removed font related customisation, including size while this work progresses. Alternative options are being worked on to bring back customization in a future update.

You can find discussions on the topic, along with draft design proposals linked below. https://thunderbird.topicbox.com/groups/ux/Ta9a415d4e3e807cf/uxd-11-26-updates-mobile-message-list

As you note, the app requires an account to be setup in order to access the settings. Without, they have little meaning. There would be no email to display the effect of font size, theming or color changes on. No server communication means notifications and other setting changes could not be tested.

One idea might be to implement a limited pre-setup demo mode with a "welcome" email that users could test accessibility settings on.

Mozilla connect is the best place to submit ideas and suggestions, which are then open to votes from the community.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
creator: platform34
